festivals.name: Tbilisoba
festivals.description: Tbilisi's city festival celebrates Georgian culture with wine, folk dancing, polyphonic singing, and artisan markets.

festivals.name: Rtveli (Wine Harvest)
festivals.description: Georgia's ancient wine harvest season sees guests invited to stomp grapes in clay qvevri vessels in the wine country of Kakheti.

destinations.name: Tbilisi
destinations.description: One of Europe's most underrated capitals has a medieval old town, rooftop sulphur baths, natural wine bars, and ancient churches.

destinations.name: Kazbegi (Stepantsminda)
destinations.description: The Gergeti Trinity Church on a 2,170m hilltop with Mount Kazbek (5,047m) towering behind — Georgia's most iconic view.

destinations.name: Kakheti Wine Region
destinations.description: The birthplace of wine 8,000 years ago, with UNESCO-listed ancient vine cultivation and spectacular hilltop monasteries.

destinations.name: Mtskheta
destinations.description: Georgia's ancient first capital has two UNESCO World Heritage churches at the confluence of the Mtkvari and Aragvi rivers.

destinations.name: Vardzia Cave Monastery
destinations.description: A 12th-century cave monastery of 600 rooms carved into a volcanic cliff face by Queen Tamar, with remarkable frescoes.

destinations.name: Upper Svaneti
destinations.description: A UNESCO mountain community of medieval defensive towers in Europe's most isolated valleys with dramatic trekking.

destinations.name: Batumi
destinations.description: A subtropical Black Sea resort city with a botanical garden, casino hotels, and subtropical Adjarian landscape.

destinations.name: David Gareja
destinations.description: A rock-hewn cave monastery complex on the semi-desert Azerbaijani border with well-preserved medieval frescoes.
